seoTitle: Jianghai Technical Support | Selection Guides, Application Notes, Design Tools | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Jianghai capacitor technical support center. Providing aluminum electrolytic, film, supercapacitor selection guides, application notes, lifespan calculation tools, FAE technical support.

faqs.question: What technical support resources does Jianghai provide?
faqs.answer: Jianghai provides comprehensive technical support resources: 1) Selection Guides: Various capacitor selection manuals including parameter descriptions, selection processes, application recommendations; 2) Application Notes: Industrial inverters, photovoltaic inverters, new energy vehicles and other application cases; 3) Design Tools: Capacitor lifespan calculators, ripple current calculation tools, balancing resistor calculation tools; 4) Simulation Models: SPICE models, Saber models for circuit simulation; 5) FAE Support: Field application engineers provide technical consultation, solution design, problem resolution; 6) Sample Support: Free samples provided for testing and verification; 7) Training Services: Regular technical training and seminars.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized technical support and selection guidance for your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: How to calculate the expected lifespan of aluminum electrolytic capacitors?
faqs.answer: Jianghai aluminum electrolytic capacitor lifespan calculation formula: Lx = L0 x 2^((T0-Tx)/10) x (V0/Vx)^n. Where L0 is rated lifespan (hours), T0 is rated temperature (usually 85C or 105C), Tx is actual working temperature, V0 is rated voltage, Vx is actual working voltage, n is voltage coefficient (usually 2-3). Example: CD139 series rated lifespan 10,000 hours at 85C, actual working temperature 65C, working voltage 400V, then expected lifespan is approximately 10,000 x 2^((85-65)/10) x (450/400)^2.5 ≈ 40,000 hours. Jianghai provides online lifespan calculation tools for quick estimation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online lifespan calculator or contact FAE for detailed lifespan analysis based on your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: How to obtain Jianghai capacitor reliability data?
faqs.answer: Jianghai capacitor reliability data can be obtained through: 1) Product Datasheets: Include basic reliability parameters such as lifespan, temperature range, ripple current; 2) Reliability Reports: Provide detailed reliability test data including high-temperature lifespan testing, temperature cycling testing, humidity testing, vibration testing; 3) MTBF Data: Industrial-grade products MTBF over 100,000 hours; 4) Failure Mode Analysis: Provide main failure modes and preventive measures; 5) Quality Assurance: ISO9001, IATF16949 quality management system certifications. For detailed reliability data, contact BeiLuo or Jianghai FAE team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request detailed reliability reports from our sales team for your specific product requirements and application conditions.

articles.title: Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitor Selection Guide

author.name: Jianghai FAE Team
author.title: Senior Field Application Engineer - Power Electronics
author.bio: Senior FAE with 15+ years of experience in capacitor applications, specializing in power electronics, industrial inverters, and renewable energy systems. Expert in aluminum electrolytic capacitor selection and reliability analysis.

articles.summary: Detailed introduction to Jianghai aluminum electrolytic capacitor selection methods, including parameter interpretation, selection steps, application notes, and FAQ.
content: This technical reference document provides detailed information about jianghai product specifications, characteristics, and performance parameters. Use this information to support your design and analysis activities.

Electrical characteristics are specified over the operating temperature range unless otherwise noted. Parameters are guaranteed by design, testing, or statistical analysis. Typical values represent the most likely parametric norm at 25°C.

Thermal characteristics require careful attention during system design. The junction-to-ambient thermal resistance depends on the mounting configuration, PCB copper area, and airflow conditions. Use thermal simulation tools to predict operating temperatures under actual conditions.

Reliability data is based on accelerated life testing and field failure analysis. Mean time between failures (MTBF) calculations follow industry-standard methodologies. Contact BeiLuo for detailed reliability reports and qualification data.

downloads.title: Jianghai Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitor Selection Guide

faeInsights.insight: The most common issues in aluminum electrolytic capacitor selection are improper capacitance and voltage selection. Through years of field support experience, we found that 60% of early capacitor failures are due to insufficient voltage margin or inadequate ripple current capability. For industrial applications, we recommend maintaining at least 1.25x voltage margin and calculating ripple current based on actual operating conditions with 20% safety margin. Temperature is the most critical factor affecting lifespan - every 10°C reduction can double the expected lifespan. In high-power applications, thermal management should be the primary design consideration.
faeInsights.logic: The selection process follows a systematic approach: 1) Determine voltage rating based on maximum operating voltage with safety margin; 2) Calculate required capacitance based on ripple current and voltage ripple requirements; 3) Verify ripple current capability at actual operating temperature; 4) Evaluate physical dimensions and mounting requirements; 5) Assess lifespan requirements and select appropriate temperature rating (85°C or 105°C).
keyTakeaways: Maintain 1.25x voltage margin for reliable operation
Ripple current is a key parameter - must meet actual application requirements
Temperature is the primary factor affecting capacitor lifespan
Every 10°C temperature reduction doubles expected lifespan
Proper heat dissipation design is critical for long-term reliability
commonPitfalls: Selecting voltage rating too close to operating voltage
Ignoring ripple current requirements in high-frequency applications
Inadequate heat dissipation design near heat-generating components
Not considering temperature derating for long-life applications
bestPractices: Use 105°C rated capacitors for high-temperature environments
Maintain 10mm+ spacing between capacitors for heat dissipation
Place capacitors away from IGBT and inductor heat sources
Implement forced air cooling for high-power applications
Regular thermal monitoring in critical applications
troubleshootingTips: Measure actual operating temperature with thermocouple
Check ripple current with current probe under full load
Verify voltage margin with oscilloscope during switching
Inspect for bulging or venting signs of overstress
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitor Selection Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: Capacitor early failure occurring within 2 years, lifespan not meeting 8-year design expectation
customerCases.diagnosis: On-site analysis revealed capacitors operating at 75°C ambient due to poor heat dissipation layout, exceeding rated ripple current by 30%
customerCases.solution: Reselected according to FAE guidance: increased voltage margin from 1.1x to 1.3x, implemented improved heat sink design, relocated capacitors away from IGBT module
customerCases.results: Capacitor operating temperature reduced to 55°C, lifespan extended from 2 years to 10+ years, meeting design goals with 25% margin

articles.title: Film Capacitor Selection and Application Guide

author.name: Jianghai FAE Team
author.title: Senior Field Application Engineer - Power Electronics
author.bio: Senior FAE with 15+ years of experience in capacitor applications, specializing in power electronics, industrial inverters, and renewable energy systems. Expert in film capacitor technology and DC-Link applications.

articles.summary: Comprehensive introduction to Jianghai film capacitor characteristics, selection points, application scenarios, and comparison analysis with aluminum electrolytic capacitors.
content: This technical reference document provides detailed information about jianghai product specifications, characteristics, and performance parameters. Use this information to support your design and analysis activities.

Electrical characteristics are specified over the operating temperature range unless otherwise noted. Parameters are guaranteed by design, testing, or statistical analysis. Typical values represent the most likely parametric norm at 25°C.

Thermal characteristics require careful attention during system design. The junction-to-ambient thermal resistance depends on the mounting configuration, PCB copper area, and airflow conditions. Use thermal simulation tools to predict operating temperatures under actual conditions.

Reliability data is based on accelerated life testing and field failure analysis. Mean time between failures (MTBF) calculations follow industry-standard methodologies. Contact BeiLuo for detailed reliability reports and qualification data.

faeInsights.insight: Film capacitors replacing aluminum electrolytic capacitors in photovoltaic inverters is an industry trend driven by the need for 20+ year warranty periods. The key selection parameter is dv/dt capability, which must exceed the actual IGBT dv/dt to prevent capacitor damage during switching. Film capacitors offer 30-50% capacitance reduction compared to aluminum electrolytic due to superior ripple current handling. The self-healing property of metallized film technology provides inherent reliability advantages - minor dielectric breakdowns heal automatically without catastrophic failure.
faeInsights.logic: Film capacitor selection prioritizes: 1) dv/dt rating must exceed IGBT switching characteristics; 2) Voltage rating with 10-15% margin for reliability; 3) Ripple current capability based on inverter output requirements; 4) ESR and ESL for high-frequency performance; 5) Physical integration with power module layout.
keyTakeaways: dv/dt is a critical parameter for IGBT applications
Capacitance can be 30-50% smaller than aluminum electrolytic equivalents
Self-healing provides automatic recovery from minor breakdowns
100,000+ hour lifespan ideal for long-warranty applications
Lower ESR reduces power losses and improves efficiency
commonPitfalls: Insufficient dv/dt rating for fast-switching IGBTs
Ignoring ESL effects in high-frequency snubber applications
Inadequate mounting torque causing mechanical stress
Not accounting for voltage transients in DC-Link applications
bestPractices: Select dv/dt at least 20% above IGBT maximum switching rate
Use proper torque during installation to avoid case deformation
Minimize connection inductance for snubber applications
Implement voltage derating for high-reliability designs
Consider parallel connection for high ripple current requirements
troubleshootingTips: Measure actual dv/dt with high-bandwidth oscilloscope
Check for corona discharge signs at high voltage stress
Verify mounting torque with calibrated torque wrench
Monitor case temperature during full-load operation
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this Film Capacitor Selection and Application Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: Original aluminum electrolytic capacitor lifespan insufficient, unable to meet 20-year warranty requirement, frequent warranty claims
customerCases.diagnosis: Aluminum electrolytic capacitors limited to 10,000-15,000 hours at operating temperature, requiring multiple replacements over system lifetime
customerCases.solution: Switched to Jianghai DC-Link film capacitors with 100,000 hour rated lifespan, optimized DC-Link busbar layout for reduced inductance
customerCases.results: System lifespan reached 25+ years, efficiency improved 0.8%, volume reduced 40%, warranty claims eliminated

articles.title: Supercapacitor Selection and Application Guide

author.name: Jianghai FAE Team
author.title: Senior Field Application Engineer - Energy Storage
author.bio: Senior FAE specializing in energy storage solutions with expertise in supercapacitor applications, power management, and industrial backup systems.

articles.summary: Detailed introduction to Jianghai EDLC and lithium-ion supercapacitor characteristics, selection methods, charge/discharge management, and typical application cases.
content: This technical reference document provides detailed information about jianghai product specifications, characteristics, and performance parameters. Use this information to support your design and analysis activities.

Electrical characteristics are specified over the operating temperature range unless otherwise noted. Parameters are guaranteed by design, testing, or statistical analysis. Typical values represent the most likely parametric norm at 25°C.

Thermal characteristics require careful attention during system design. The junction-to-ambient thermal resistance depends on the mounting configuration, PCB copper area, and airflow conditions. Use thermal simulation tools to predict operating temperatures under actual conditions.

Reliability data is based on accelerated life testing and field failure analysis. Mean time between failures (MTBF) calculations follow industry-standard methodologies. Contact BeiLuo for detailed reliability reports and qualification data.

faeInsights.insight: Supercapacitor selection requires careful energy and power calculations. The fundamental formulas are Energy E=1/2×C×V² and Power P=V²/(4×ESR). A critical but often overlooked requirement is cell balancing when using series configurations - without proper balancing, voltage imbalance can cause overvoltage damage to individual cells. Self-discharge characteristics must also be considered - EDLC typically loses about 20% charge per day, making them unsuitable for long-term energy storage but ideal for short-term backup and pulse power applications.
faeInsights.logic: Supercapacitor system design follows: 1) Calculate energy requirement based on backup time or pulse energy; 2) Determine voltage requirement and series cell count; 3) Size capacitance based on energy and voltage constraints; 4) Verify power capability through ESR analysis; 5) Implement cell balancing circuit for series configurations; 6) Design thermal management for high-current applications.
keyTakeaways: Energy calculation: E=1/2×C×V² is fundamental to sizing
Cell balancing is mandatory for series configurations
Self-discharge rate (~20%/day) limits long-term storage applications
ESR determines maximum power delivery capability
500,000+ cycle life enables maintenance-free operation
commonPitfalls: Neglecting cell balancing in series configurations
Underestimating self-discharge for backup time calculations
Insufficient current rating for high-power pulse applications
Overlooking temperature effects on ESR and capacitance
bestPractices: Always implement active or passive cell balancing
Size for 20% extra capacity to account for self-discharge
Monitor cell temperatures in high-current applications
Use appropriate wire sizing for peak current capability
Implement overvoltage protection for each cell
troubleshootingTips: Measure individual cell voltages to check balancing effectiveness
Monitor temperature rise during charge/discharge cycles
Verify ESR with ESR meter to detect cell degradation
Check for electrolyte leakage signs in cylindrical cells
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this Supercapacitor Selection and Application Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: Need to maintain microcontroller operation 2-3 minutes after power failure to complete data saving and secure communication
customerCases.diagnosis: Battery backup presented maintenance challenges and temperature limitations for outdoor meter installations
customerCases.solution: Used Jianghai 300F/2.7V supercapacitor with integrated balancing and charging circuit, providing maintenance-free backup power
customerCases.results: Power-fail hold time reached 3.5 minutes, cycle life over 500,000 cycles, operating temperature range -40°C to +65°C, completely replacing batteries

articles.title: Inverter Capacitor Application Design Guide

author.name: Jianghai FAE Team
author.title: Principal Field Application Engineer - Industrial Drives
author.bio: Principal FAE with 20+ years of experience in industrial drive systems, specializing in inverter design, thermal management, and reliability optimization.

articles.summary: Detailed introduction to various capacitor application designs in inverters, including DC-Link capacitor calculation, snubber circuit design, balancing circuit design, heat dissipation design, etc.
content: This technical reference document provides detailed information about jianghai product specifications, characteristics, and performance parameters. Use this information to support your design and analysis activities.

Electrical characteristics are specified over the operating temperature range unless otherwise noted. Parameters are guaranteed by design, testing, or statistical analysis. Typical values represent the most likely parametric norm at 25°C.

Thermal characteristics require careful attention during system design. The junction-to-ambient thermal resistance depends on the mounting configuration, PCB copper area, and airflow conditions. Use thermal simulation tools to predict operating temperatures under actual conditions.

Reliability data is based on accelerated life testing and field failure analysis. Mean time between failures (MTBF) calculations follow industry-standard methodologies. Contact BeiLuo for detailed reliability reports and qualification data.

faeInsights.insight: In inverter capacitor design, heat dissipation is the most easily overlooked issue yet the most critical factor for long-term reliability. Aluminum electrolytic capacitor lifespan is extremely sensitive to temperature - the well-known rule of thumb is that lifespan halves for every 10°C temperature increase. Through extensive field analysis, we found that 70% of capacitor failures in industrial inverters are thermally related. Strategic placement, adequate spacing, and proper thermal management can extend capacitor life by 3-5x.
faeInsights.logic: Inverter capacitor design requires integrated thermal and electrical analysis: 1) Calculate DC-Link capacitance based on voltage ripple and hold-up time requirements; 2) Size snubber capacitors based on IGBT switching characteristics and stray inductance; 3) Perform thermal analysis to predict operating temperatures; 4) Implement physical layout optimization for heat dissipation; 5) Select temperature-appropriate capacitor ratings based on thermal analysis results.
keyTakeaways: Heat dissipation is the key to long capacitor life in inverters
Lifespan halves for every 10°C temperature increase
Maintain 10mm+ spacing between capacitors for airflow
Avoid placing capacitors near IGBT and reactor heat sources
Use 105°C rated capacitors for high-temperature environments
commonPitfalls: Insufficient thermal analysis leading to underestimated temperatures
Capacitors placed too close to heat-generating components
Inadequate airflow in enclosed inverter enclosures
bestPractices: Perform thermal simulation during design phase
Use 105°C long-life capacitors for industrial applications
Implement forced air cooling for high-power inverters
Monitor capacitor temperatures during prototype testing
Design for worst-case ambient plus safety margin
troubleshootingTips: Use infrared camera to identify hot spots in capacitor bank
Measure air temperature at capacitor inlet and outlet
Check for blocked air filters or cooling fan failures
Verify capacitor surface temperature with thermocouples
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this Inverter Capacitor Application Design Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: Inverter capacitors frequently failed with average lifespan only 2 years, causing elevator downtime and maintenance costs
customerCases.diagnosis: Thermal analysis revealed capacitor bank operating at 80°C due to proximity to IGBT module and inadequate airflow in compact enclosure
customerCases.solution: Optimized heat dissipation design: increased capacitor spacing to 15mm, relocated capacitors away from IGBT, added dedicated cooling duct, selected 105°C long-life capacitors
customerCases.results: Capacitor operating temperature reduced to 55°C, lifespan extended to 10+ years, product return rate reduced 85%, maintenance costs decreased significantly
